concepts.label: Media system
concepts.definition: An umbrella system for producing, controlling, storing, playing or distributing media whose primary function still needs to be resolved.

language.question: Which media function is primary: production, broadcast, distribution, streaming, physical playback, interactive installation, control, or archive and access?

concepts.label: Media production system
concepts.definition: A system used to capture, create or transform media.

concepts.label: Broadcast system
concepts.definition: A system carrying programme material into a live or scheduled broadcast output.

concepts.label: Media distribution system
concepts.definition: A system for routing and delivering media across destinations.

concepts.label: Streaming platform
concepts.definition: A digital platform that prepares and delivers media streams.

concepts.label: Physical playback system
concepts.definition: Installed equipment that reproduces media in a physical environment.

concepts.label: Interactive media installation
concepts.definition: A physical experience whose media responds to people, state or environment.

concepts.label: Media-control system
concepts.definition: A system coordinating media devices, routing and operational control.

concepts.label: Media archive platform
concepts.definition: A structured platform for retaining and accessing media.

concepts.label: Community platform
concepts.definition: An umbrella digital service supporting participation, publishing, membership or shared resources for a community.

language.question: Is the primary community function publishing, membership, participation, shared resources, or a combination?

concepts.label: Community publishing platform
concepts.definition: A community service centred on creating and distributing shared content.

concepts.label: Community membership platform
concepts.definition: A community service centred on accounts, membership and participation.

concepts.label: Community resource platform
concepts.definition: A community service centred on discovering and sharing structured resources.

concepts.label: Digital platform
concepts.definition: An umbrella digital service whose audience, data, participation and operating model still need to be resolved.

language.question: Which platform function is primary: application, community participation, media streaming, or structured data?

concepts.label: Hardware product
concepts.definition: A physical product combining electronic, embedded, mechanical or software-controlled behaviour.

concepts.label: Content system
concepts.definition: An umbrella system for structuring, creating, managing and publishing content across destinations.

language.question: Is the primary content function editorial production, multi-format publishing, archive and access, or a combination?

concepts.label: Scalability
concepts.definition: The ability of a system to grow in users, data, delivery volume or operating reach.

concepts.label: Accessibility
concepts.definition: The quality of remaining usable and understandable across differing access needs.

concepts.label: Security
concepts.definition: Protection of access, information and operation against unauthorised or unsafe use.

concepts.label: Organisational identity
concepts.definition: An identity system representing an organisation.

concepts.label: Product identity
concepts.definition: An identity system representing a product or product family.

concepts.label: Visual-language system
concepts.definition: A repeatable visual language governing recognition and expression.

concepts.label: Operational data system
concepts.definition: A data system centred on live operational records.

concepts.label: Knowledge system
concepts.definition: A structured system for connecting, interpreting and retrieving knowledge.

concepts.label: Analytics system
concepts.definition: A data system centred on measurement and interpretation.

concepts.label: Editorial content system
concepts.definition: A system supporting editorial creation and governance.

concepts.label: Multi-format publishing system
concepts.definition: A system publishing structured content across multiple formats.

concepts.label: Content archive system
concepts.definition: A system retaining and exposing archived content.

concepts.label: Engineering role
concepts.definition: A request to discover engineering capabilities relevant to another resolved object, medium or environment.

concepts.label: Design role
concepts.definition: A request to discover design capabilities relevant to another resolved object, medium or environment.

concepts.label: Audio engineering
concepts.definition: An engineering capability family spanning sound systems, signal flow, recording, broadcast and live audio.

concepts.label: Studio engineering
concepts.definition: Audio engineering and technical signal-flow work within a studio environment.

concepts.label: Studio environment
concepts.definition: A controlled environment for recording, production, monitoring or technical media work.

concepts.label: Music
concepts.definition: Music as a creative, production and technical domain.

concepts.label: Audio
concepts.definition: Sound treated as a technical medium, production material or delivery signal.

concepts.label: Field recording
concepts.definition: Capturing audio within a live or location environment.

concepts.label: Design
concepts.definition: Defining form, behaviour, structure or experience before implementation.

concepts.label: Build
concepts.definition: Implementing or assembling a defined digital or physical system.

concepts.label: Install
concepts.definition: Placing, connecting and commissioning a system in its operating environment.

concepts.label: Repair
concepts.definition: Diagnosing and correcting a fault in an existing system.

concepts.label: Configure
concepts.definition: Setting system relationships and operating parameters.

concepts.label: Operate
concepts.definition: Running and supporting a system in active use.

concepts.label: Make
concepts.definition: A general request to cause or create a result whose precise activity depends on the requested target or state.

concepts.label: Transition to live operation
concepts.definition: Moving a system or service from preparation into its public or operational state.

concepts.label: Access control
concepts.definition: Defining and enforcing permission, trust and security boundaries around systems and data.

concepts.label: Migrate
concepts.definition: Moving a system, service or information set between operating environments.

concepts.label: Recreate
concepts.definition: Reconstructing an existing visual or technical artefact from surviving source material.

concepts.label: Server system
concepts.definition: An ambiguous server request that may concern physical hardware, server software or a hosted runtime.

language.question: Is this a physical server, server software, or a hosted runtime?

concepts.label: Physical server
concepts.definition: Physical compute equipment installed and connected within technical infrastructure.

concepts.label: Server software
concepts.definition: Software providing a networked service or application runtime.

concepts.label: Server rack
concepts.definition: A physical rack environment for equipment layout, power, cooling, cabling and networking.

concepts.label: Logo artwork
concepts.definition: A mark or logo treated as a specific visual artefact rather than an entire identity system.

concepts.label: Logo and identity design
concepts.definition: Design capability for marks, visual language and identity applications.

concepts.label: Source image
concepts.definition: Existing image material supplied as the reference for transformation or reconstruction.

concepts.label: Communications system
concepts.definition: An operational system carrying communications between connected participants or endpoints.

concepts.label: Live environment
concepts.definition: An operating context where technical decisions and delivery occur in real time.

concepts.label: Operational state
concepts.definition: A system or service active in its intended operating context.

concepts.label: Ecommerce storefront
concepts.definition: A public digital commerce surface supporting product discovery and purchase journeys.

concepts.label: Checkout system
concepts.definition: A transaction component handling basket, checkout and purchase completion within a wider digital service.

concepts.label: Product catalogue
concepts.definition: A structured collection and presentation of product information, independent of whether transactions are supported.
